Technical Mechanism: DAG-structured routing and Router/Executor dual-role architecture across all agent nodes.
Strategic Innovation: Transitions from traditional centralized/isolated models to a collaboration-centric approach using dynamic Directed Acyclic Graphs for coordinating complex dependencies.
Primary Benefit: Enhances scalability, fault tolerance, and reliability through autonomous coordination and emergent collective intelligence without single points of failure.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): LLM backbone (e.g., GPT-4), coordination protocol, and independent routing logic per node.

Technical Mechanism: Vector-based databases, persistent long-term memory pools, and BM25 search rebuilt from flat files.
Strategic Innovation: Moves beyond simple semantic similarity by modeling documents as dependency graphs and decoupling canonical storage from retrieval speed to support multihop reasoning.
Primary Benefit: Mitigates context rot and reduces hallucinations by grounding decisions in extensive external knowledge while maintaining human-readable transparency.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Specific memory pool configurations (e.g., vector DBs), SQLite with vector extensions, and LLM orchestrators.

Technical Mechanism: Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) network using selective DFS traversal and adaptive weighting/edge pruning based on execution observations.
Strategic Innovation: Replaces static, hardcoded workflows and role assignments with real-time reconfiguration that evolves based on environment demands and agent performance.
Primary Benefit: Significant scalability in processing diverse real-world tasks while optimizing information flow and reducing overhead.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Python interpreter, LLM backbones, dynamic weight matrix maintenance, and Standard Operational Procedure (SOP) sets.

Technical Mechanism: Retrieval-based mechanism where agents maintain fixed-size pools ( $M_{rou}$  and  $M_{exe}$ ) of successful task trajectories.
Strategic Innovation: Facilitates continuous specialization through few-shot learning from local experiences instead of relying on global static role definitions.
Primary Benefit: Promotes scalable expertise refinement and safeguards intellectual property through local data storage and privacy-preserving collaboration.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Limited-capacity memory modules and semantic embedding functions (e.g., BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5).

Technical Mechanism: Internal agent workflow containing a Router for analyzing queries/routing and an Executor for operations/tools.
Strategic Innovation: Encapsulates the 'plan vs execute' logic within every individual node rather than relying on a centralized planning agent.
Primary Benefit: Supports autonomous task propagation, local expertise development, and better load balancing across the swarm.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Substantial LLM backbone per agent (e.g., DeepSeek-V3 or GPT-4o-mini).

Technical Mechanism: Agents utilize a Finite State Machine to perform Forward ( $O_{fwd}$ ), Split ( $O_{split}$ ), or Execute ( $O_{exec}$ ) operations.
Strategic Innovation: Enables flexible, asynchronous collaboration without cyclic dependencies, moving away from rigid serial workflows.
Primary Benefit: Prevents infinite loops and optimizes workload distribution based on dynamically updated agent capability vectors.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Router module capable of task decomposition reasoning and similarity functions for requirement matching.

Technical Mechanism: Externalized flat file storage (MEMORY.md, PROJECT.md) used as the primary state source.
Strategic Innovation: Moves state out of the LLM context window to external files to prevent context rot and "amnesia."
Primary Benefit: Achieves 15% better token efficiency than JSON and ensures human-readable/editable transparency for the source of truth.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Flat file repository structure (/docs) and Git version control.

Technical Mechanism: Logging failures in a strict 'Scenario -> Attempt -> Solution' format with attribute-based tagging.
Strategic Innovation: Specifically retrieves past failures ("negative knowledge") to prevent repeated mistakes, addressing a common gap in multi-agent systems.
Primary Benefit: Increases reliability and compute efficiency by avoiding known-bad implementation paths.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Custom taxonomy for failure categories and Wilson score interval for voting.

Technical Mechanism: Orchestrator dispatches sub-agents (Threads) to complete single actions in isolation with compressed returns.
Strategic Innovation: Replaces chaotic multi-agent chat with OS-style isolated execution and compressed 'Episode' returns.
Primary Benefit: Prevents token explosion and context contamination across agent swarms.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Stateless worker process management and Episodic Return compression logic.

Technical Mechanism: Lightweight routing agent searches a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for specific tool selection.
Strategic Innovation: Implements dynamic tool loading based on task requirements instead of providing full toolsets to every agent.
Primary Benefit: Massive token savings and significant reduction in tool-related hallucinations.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Model Context Protocol (MCP) server and a lightweight Tool Selector prompt.

Technical Mechanism: Simultaneous querying of diverse models (Claude, GPT, Gemini) with majority voting and entropy scoring.
Strategic Innovation: Uses multi-model disagreement as a tool for uncertainty and hallucination detection.
Primary Benefit: Mathematical reduction of hallucinations and increased reliability in high-stakes tasks.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Multi-model API access and semantic clustering logic.

Technical Mechanism: Mandatory termination of agent processes upon task completion or context saturation.
Strategic Innovation: Ensures agents always start with a clean context window, reading only relevant data from external state.
Primary Benefit: Eliminates performance degradation caused by 'lost-in-the-middle' effects and context bloat.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Deterministic orchestrator script to handle process kill-and-restart cycles.

Technical Mechanism: High-fidelity context compression (20:1 ratio) using structured JSON schemas for session handoffs.
Strategic Innovation: Captures 'decisions over discussion,' explicitly recording rejected ideas and risks often lost in standard summaries.
Primary Benefit: Maintains 97% reconstruction fidelity for long-horizon projects while drastically saving context window space.
Implementation Requirement (Inferred): Compression/Decompression agent prompts and UUID v4 packet tracking.
